message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1484,8 +1484,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.2","title":"I2: instrument split-path taxonomy, hold/wait distributions, copy-allocation churn, and cache-hit truthing","description":"## Goal
Measure the second layer of truthing data needed to separate structural convoy from baseline waste.

## Why This Exists
The revised plan needs to know not only why retries happen, but also how often inserts use local insert vs local split vs right-edge split vs broad rebalance; how long shared surfaces are held; how much copy/allocation churn occurs; and whether statement/record/page-buffer caches are actually hitting.

## Scope
- count split-path usage: pure leaf-local insert, local leaf split, right-edge split, full non-root rebalance, root split
- capture median and p99 hold/wait duration for parent/internal surfaces, allocator/page-1 surfaces, and structural waits per successful transaction
- record staged full-page copies, bytes copied, heap allocations, PageData ownership conversions, and record decodes per transaction on representative rows
- measure statement-plan cache hit rate, cursor reuse rate, decode-cache hit rate, and page-buffer pool hit rate where those facilities exist

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- representative rows expose split-path counts, hold/wait distributions, copy/allocation counters, and cache-hit metrics in stable artifacts
- unit tests cover counter aggregation and edge cases such as zero-hit rows or no-wait rows
- the instrumentation is lightweight enough that focused benchmark reruns remain usable for optimization steering","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- representative rows expose split-path counts, hold/wait distributions, copy/allocation counters, and cache-hit metrics in stable artifacts
- unit tests cover counter aggregation and edge cases such as zero-hit rows or no-wait rows
- the instrumentation is lightweight enough that focused benchmark reruns remain usable for optimization steering","notes":"Coordination note:
I2 owns the structural-side truthing, but it should share counters and artifact layout with J1 rather than blocking the baseline-tax truthing lane. The design intent is parallel measurement after the minimal A3 leafs unlock.

Verification/logging requirement:
- require a benchmark-output smoke check proving these fields appear on the focused bad row and at least one mixed row
- record stable structured log families for split_path_summary, hold_wait_distribution, copy_alloc_churn, and cache_hit_ledger so later implementation beads can cite them directly","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:53.900701018Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:07.927478087Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocation","cache","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-i","evidence","measurement","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:53.900701018Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:06.181574737Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2286,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P0.3, P0.4, P0.5, and O.1.
- Supplies the split-path, hold/wait, and copy/allocation truthing needed before structural surgery.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.3","title":"I3: truth workload semantics for disjoint inserts and publish the revised conflict geometry note","description":"## Goal
Stop trusting benchmark labels and explicitly document what the worst workloads actually mean physically.

## Scope
- establish whether keys and rowids are monotone, whether secondary indexes are touched, and whether inserts converge on the same right-most leaf or parent chain
- tie benchmark SQL shape to actual page-space conflict surfaces using I1 and I2 data
- write the conflict-geometry note that future agents can consult instead of re-deriving the workload shape from code
- explicitly call out which conflicts are fundamental versus accidental for the focused bad row and one mixed row

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the disjoint-insert workload has an explicit physical conflict description that future agents can cite directly from the bead
- the note distinguishes true hot-leaf contention, right-edge growth, parent/internal convoy, allocator/page-1 false sharing, and publication surfaces
- at least one mixed row receives the same treatment so the structural story is not overfit to a single benchmark family
- the deliverable includes a compact SQL-shape summary, a page-touch-class summary, and a clear prioritization takeaway for later structural beads","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the disjoint-insert workload has an explicit physical conflict description that future agents can cite directly from the bead
- the note distinguishes true hot-leaf contention, right-edge growth, parent/internal convoy, allocator/page-1 false sharing, and publication surfaces
- at least one mixed row receives the same treatment so the structural story is not overfit to a single benchmark family
- the deliverable includes a compact SQL-shape summary, a page-touch-class summary, and a clear prioritization takeaway for later structural beads","notes":"Deliverable requirement:
- close this bead with a compact, self-contained conflict-geometry packet: workload SQL/transaction summary, physical page-touch summary, retry-cause summary, and a short statement of what that means for prioritization
- the output should be legible to someone who has not read the benchmark code recently and should directly answer whether the row is truly disjoint, append-heavy, parent-convoy-heavy, allocator-heavy, or mixed","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:03.923933112Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:08.457911358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-i","evidence","realdb","right-edge","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:03.923933112Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:27.027715310Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:37.261247514Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2287,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers WC.3, P0.6, and DG.1.
- This is the bead that determines whether the bad row is truly generic parent convoy or really right-edge append geometry in disguise.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.4","title":"I4: design extent leases and header or freelist delta reconciliation for the ordinary insert path","description":"## Goal
Design the first-wave allocator decoupling scheme that removes page-1 and freelist metadata from the ordinary insert fast path.

## Scope
- define a first-wave extent lease abstraction, including refill and return semantics
- specify how dbsize growth, freelist consumption/return, header change counters, and related metadata become logical deltas rather than ordinary page writes
- spell out crash-safety, rollback, and compatibility constraints
- include a crisp decision note on whether the first implementation should be per-transaction or per-writer leased allocation
- record the second-wave branch points for epoch-style allocation, key-range/page-range affinity hints, and append-aware growth surfaces so they do not disappear from the design

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- a concrete first-wave allocator design is documented with lease semantics, delta reconciliation rules, rollback behavior, and crash-safety constraints
- the design explicitly explains how ordinary inserts avoid touching page 1 on the fast path
- the design includes a failure/recovery matrix covering lease exhaustion, refill, rollback, crash/restart, and returned-unused-space handling
- the design records when epoch allocator refinement, key-range/page-range affinity, and append-aware growth surfaces should be considered as later phases instead of leaving them implicit
- the design is specific enough to guide implementation without reopening the markdown plan","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- a concrete first-wave allocator design is documented with lease semantics, delta reconciliation rules, rollback behavior, and crash-safety constraints
- the design explicitly explains how ordinary inserts avoid touching page 1 on the fast path
- the design includes a failure/recovery matrix covering lease exhaustion, refill, rollback, crash/restart, and returned-unused-space handling
- the design records when epoch allocator refinement, key-range/page-range affinity, and append-aware growth surfaces should be considered as later phases instead of leaving them implicit
- the design is specific enough to guide implementation without reopening the markdown plan","notes":"Design-packet requirement:
- record the chosen lease ownership model, refill/return rules, delta reconciliation boundaries, and on-disk compatibility invariants
- include the first-wave recommendation explicitly: start with one lease per writer/transaction, reserve a modest extent (roughly 32-128 pages unless evidence says otherwise), represent it concretely (for example ExtentLease { start, len, next }), and refill rarely rather than rediscovering allocator state on each split
- include at least one worked example each for: normal lease refill, rollback after partial lease consumption, commit reconciliation, and append-biased growth where a right-edge-heavy workload is detected
- explicitly call out which metadata remains fundamental shared state and which metadata is being removed from the ordinary insert path
- capture the second-wave decision gates for epoch allocator refinement and for optional key-range/page-range affinity hints so future agents know when those ideas become justified rather than rediscovering them from scratch","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:13.916353876Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:08.977409537Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","db300-program","db300-track-i","design","mvcc","page1","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:57.511358797Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:13.916353876Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:17.270200524Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:47.437760165Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2288,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers WC.1, NP.3, NP.6, NP.7, P1.1-P1.5, O.2, and STRAT.1 at the allocator-design level.
- It also captures the second-wave branch points for P1.3 epoch allocation and P1.4/P1.5 affinity/append-aware routing.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:46Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.9.5","title":"I5: implement extent leases and remove page-1 or freelist work from the ordinary insert fast path","description":"## Goal
Land the first working allocator decoupling change from I4.

## Scope
- reserve small extents or equivalent local allocation buffers on demand
- satisfy ordinary split/growth allocation from the local lease instead of touching page-1/freelist metadata on every growth event
- stage header/freelist effects as logical deltas reconciled later in a narrow finalization path
- include append-aware allocation behavior when workload truthing shows right-edge-heavy growth, without turning it into a correctness requirement
- add unit tests for lease exhaustion, refill, rollback, and commit reconciliation
- add focused e2e and benchmark runs proving page-1/header touches fall sharply without introducing new structural locking

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the ordinary insert fast path no longer requires page-1/freelist mutation on each growth event
- focused artifacts show a large drop in page-1/header touches and allocator-related retries on insert-heavy rows
- allocator-related retries become a minority of total retry pressure on the focused bad row unless another clearly-documented accidental surface dominates
- unit tests, rollback tests, and e2e validation cover lease refill, exhaustion, rollback, commit reconciliation, and append-aware lease use with rich logs","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the ordinary insert fast path no longer requires page-1/freelist mutation on each growth event
- focused artifacts show a large drop in page-1/header touches and allocator-related retries on insert-heavy rows
- allocator-related retries become a minority of total retry pressure on the focused bad row unless another clearly-documented accidental surface dominates
- unit tests, rollback tests, and e2e validation cover lease refill, exhaustion, rollback, commit reconciliation, and append-aware lease use with rich logs","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for lease exhaustion, refill, rollback, commit reconciliation, no-stale-metadata carryover, and append-aware lease selection where that branch exists
- add targeted e2e coverage on insert-heavy rows that previously touched page 1 / allocator metadata frequently
- emit structured log events or counters for allocator_lease_grant, allocator_lease_refill, allocator_delta_commit, allocator_delta_rollback, append_affinity_hint_used, and page1_touch_count so evidence can be compared mechanically
- keep key-range/page-range affinity as a hint, not a correctness dependency, and document clearly when it is active versus inactive","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:24.078233844Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:09.505473742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","db300-program","db300-track-i","hot-path","mvcc","page1","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:24.078233844Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:07.553341523Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2289,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P1.1, P1.2, P1.4, P1.5, P1.6, O.2, and DG.2 in executable form.
- This is the first-wave implementation bead for removing page-1/freelist work from the ordinary insert path.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:46Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.9.6","title":"I6: rewrite the write hot path into explicit tiers with cheap owned-page checks and reduced restart scope","description":"## Goal
Shrink the common write path so ordinary successful writes stop paying rare-path tax.

## Scope
- define and implement explicit Tier 0 already-owned, Tier 1 first-touch uncontended, and Tier 2 conflict/structural/rare paths
- use an owned-page representation optimized for common transactions, with an inline cheap path for small transactions and an upgrade path to a denser/open-addressed representation when necessary
- avoid repeated global lookup, snapshot revalidation, generic cleanup, or abstraction-layer churn on Tier 0
- replace broad hot-path lookups with more local ownership/state access where practical, such as denser page descriptors or owner/version metadata nearer the page-cache state
- concentrate heavyweight validation and cleanup in cold or rare paths
- reduce restart scope so structural losses restart from a structural planning point rather than whole transactions when correctness allows
- allow selective delta staging or other lighter-weight page staging when that is measurably cheaper than unconditional full-page copy
- explicitly isolate rare path branches behind cold helpers so the hot path does not pay branch/codegen tax for unusual cleanup and recovery work

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- Tier 0 and Tier 1 paths are explicit in code and no longer pay obvious rare-path coordination tax
- already-owned pages use a cheaper ownership test than the pre-refactor path and avoid unnecessary global registry/state churn
- representative c1 and c8 rows show reduced instructions/op before any micro-publish miracle is required
- unit and e2e tests prove correctness of owned-page reuse, selective staging choices, restart-scope reduction, and mode parity, with structured logging for tier selection","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- Tier 0 and Tier 1 paths are explicit in code and no longer pay obvious rare-path coordination tax
- already-owned pages use a cheaper ownership test than the pre-refactor path and avoid unnecessary global registry/state churn
- representative c1 and c8 rows show reduced instructions/op before any micro-publish miracle is required
- unit and e2e tests prove correctness of owned-page reuse, selective staging choices, restart-scope reduction, and mode parity, with structured logging for tier selection","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for Tier 0 already-owned reuse, Tier 1 uncontended first-touch acquisition, Tier 2 rare/conflict fallback, selective delta-versus-full-page staging correctness, and restart-scope reduction correctness
- add focused e2e coverage proving the same statement can lose a structural race without forcing whole-transaction restart when correctness permits
- emit structured log events or counters for write_tier_selection, owned_page_hit, first_touch_acquire, rare_path_fallback, structural_restart_scope, staging_mode_selected, and snapshot_validation_path
- make explicit in design/closeout how lazy snapshot invalidation, cold-path isolation, and structural-only waiting are handled so future agents do not quietly move rare-path cost back into Tier 0/1
- the closure note should state whether the chosen small-transaction owned-page representation is an inline sorted array, another inline structure, or something better, and why it beat pointer-chasing hash-based ownership tests on the measured path","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:34.217856065Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:10.056760803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-i","hot-path","mvcc","owned-pages","restart-scope","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:34.217856065Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:17.849146223Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:27.988365350Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2290,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers WC.4, WC.5, NP.4, NP.5, NP.7, P3.5 (restart boundary support), P4.1-P4.10, O.3, AVOID.2, and AVOID.3.
- This is the explicit three-tier write-path / owned-page-representation bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:47Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.5","title":"I5: implement extent leases and remove page-1 or freelist work from the ordinary insert fast path","description":"## Goal

## Scope

- add unit tests for lease exhaustion, refill, rollback, and commit reconciliation
- add focused e2e and benchmark runs proving page-1/header touches fall sharply without introducing new structural locking

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:

- focused artifacts show a large drop in page-1/header touches and allocator-related retries on insert-heavy rows
- allocator-related retries become a minority of total retry pressure on the focused bad row unless another clearly-documented accidental surface dominates
- unit tests, rollback tests, and e2e validation cover lease refill, exhaustion, rollback, commit reconciliation, and append-aware lease use with rich logs","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the ordinary insert fast path no longer requires page-1/freelist mutation on each growth event

- keep key-range/page-range affinity as a hint, not a correctness dependency, and document clearly when it is active versus inactive","status":"in_progress","assignee":"RainyWren","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:24.078233844Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-17T20:04:01Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","db300-program","db300-track-i","hot-path","mvcc","page1","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:24.078233844Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:07.553341523Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2289,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

+{"id":"bd-db300.9.6","title":"I6: rewrite the write hot path into explicit tiers with cheap owned-page checks and reduced restart scope","description":"## Goal
Shrink the common write path so ordinary successful writes stop paying rare-path tax.

## Scope
- define and implement explicit Tier 0 already-owned, Tier 1 first-touch uncontended, and Tier 2 conflict/structural/rare paths
- use an owned-page representation optimized for common transactions, with an inline cheap path for small transactions and an upgrade path to a denser/open-addressed representation when necessary
- avoid repeated global lookup, snapshot revalidation, generic cleanup, or abstraction-layer churn on Tier 0
- replace broad hot-path lookups with more local ownership/state access where practical, such as denser page descriptors or owner/version metadata nearer the page-cache state
- concentrate heavyweight validation and cleanup in cold or rare paths
- reduce restart scope so structural losses restart from a structural planning point rather than whole transactions when correctness allows
- allow selective delta staging or other lighter-weight page staging when that is measurably cheaper than unconditional full-page copy
- explicitly isolate rare path branches behind cold helpers so the hot path does not pay branch/codegen tax for unusual cleanup and recovery work

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- Tier 0 and Tier 1 paths are explicit in code and no longer pay obvious rare-path coordination tax
- already-owned pages use a cheaper ownership test than the pre-refactor path and avoid unnecessary global registry/state churn
- representative c1 and c8 rows show reduced instructions/op before any micro-publish miracle is required
- unit and e2e tests prove correctness of owned-page reuse, selective staging choices, restart-scope reduction, and mode parity, with structured logging for tier selection","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:

- add unit tests for Tier 0 already-owned reuse, Tier 1 uncontended first-touch acquisition, Tier 2 rare/conflict fallback, selective delta-versus-full-page staging correctness, and restart-scope reduction correctness
- add focused e2e coverage proving the same statement can lose a structural race without forcing whole-transaction restart when correctness permits
- emit structured log events or counters for write_tier_selection, owned_page_hit, first_touch_acquire, rare_path_fallback, structural_restart_scope, staging_mode_selected, and snapshot_validation_path

- the closure note should state whether the chosen small-transaction owned-page representation is an inline sorted array, another inline structure, or something better, and why it beat pointer-chasing hash-based ownership tests on the measured path","status":"in_progress","assignee":"RainyWren","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:34.217856065Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-17T20:04:01Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-i","hot-path","mvcc","owned-pages","restart-scope","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:34.217856065Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:17.849146223Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:27.988365350Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2290,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

 {"id":"bd-db300.9.7","title":"I7: make concurrent inserts prefer leaf-local insert and local split while suppressing broad non-root rebalance","description":"## Goal
Turn leaf locality into a hard concurrent-mode policy instead of an incidental hope.

## Scope
- suppress broad non-root rebalance on the concurrent insert path unless no safe local alternative exists
- make the policy explicit rather than buried in heuristics
- add counters and logs that prove how often broad rebalance still happens after the change
- cover correctness for local split, root split, and fallback paths with unit/property tests and focused contention e2e cases

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- concurrent insert paths strongly prefer leaf-local insert/local split and visibly reduce broad rebalance frequency on the target rows
- correctness tests cover fallback to broader repair when the local policy is genuinely insufficient
- logs and artifacts make remaining broad-rebalance cases easy to inspect","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- concurrent insert paths strongly prefer leaf-local insert/local split and visibly reduce broad rebalance frequency on the target rows
- correctness tests cover fallback to broader repair when the local policy is genuinely insufficient
- logs and artifacts make remaining broad-rebalance cases easy to inspect","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit/property tests covering local split correctness, broad-rebalance suppression, and safe fallback when local repair is insufficient
- add focused contention e2e runs that report broad_rebalance_count and local_split_count before and after the change
- keep logs rich enough that future agents can inspect the remaining broad-rebalance cases rather than treating them as black-box retries","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:44.345116754Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:10.626705963Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","concurrency","db300-program","db300-track-i","right-edge","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:44.345116754Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:38.116638484Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:27.796471467Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:48.335759410Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2291,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers NP.2, P2.1, P2.8, and O.4.
- This is the hard policy bead that says concurrent inserts should be split-only/local-first, not rebalance-first.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.8","title":"I8: add concurrency-aware slack, asymmetric right-edge split policy, pre-split threshold, dynamic fill factor, and deferred cleanup","description":"## Goal
Keep inserts leaf-local longer by changing the physical split policy rather than just coordinating around the old one.

## Scope
- lower occupancy targets where concurrency pressure makes compactness harmful
- use asymmetric right-edge splits so the fresh right-most page gets the slack in append-like workloads
- add a right-edge pre-split threshold that acts before the hottest leaf is fully saturated
- allow contention-adaptive fill factors for hot tables/regions when retry or parent-version-change rates rise
- support private would-be sibling or phantom-leaf staging for multi-insert transactions when that safely amortizes structural work
- permit temporary structural untidiness with deferred/background cleanup rather than forcing compactness on the hot concurrent path

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- focused contention rows show lower broad-rebalance frequency and lower right-edge or parent-related retry pressure
- unit/property tests cover asymmetric split correctness, phantom-leaf or private-sibling staging safety where used, deferred-cleanup safety, and contention-adaptive thresholds
- structured logs expose when the engine enters adaptive low-fill, append-aware pre-split, phantom-leaf staging, or deferred-cleanup behavior
- append-heavy rows stay leaf-local longer without creating hidden correctness debt or unbounded cleanup backlog","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- focused contention rows show lower broad-rebalance frequency and lower right-edge or parent-related retry pressure
- unit/property tests cover asymmetric split correctness, phantom-leaf or private-sibling staging safety where used, deferred-cleanup safety, and contention-adaptive thresholds
- structured logs expose when the engine enters adaptive low-fill, append-aware pre-split, phantom-leaf staging, or deferred-cleanup behavior
- append-heavy rows stay leaf-local longer without creating hidden correctness debt or unbounded cleanup backlog","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit/property tests for asymmetric right-edge splits, adaptive fill-factor changes, phantom-leaf/private-sibling staging safety, deferred cleanup safety, and threshold hysteresis or stability behavior
- make the split policy explicit about occupancy shape: internal pages may remain intentionally sparser than leaves, and hot/right-edge leaves may reserve extra slack so the new right-most page starts with room while the old page stays relatively fuller
- add focused e2e runs on append-like and non-append-like rows so the policy is not validated only on one geometry
- emit structured log events or counters for right_edge_presplit, adaptive_fill_factor_state, phantom_leaf_stage_used, deferred_cleanup_backlog, split_policy_choice, and deferred_cleanup_checkpoint_repair
- keep deferred cleanup explicitly bounded and observable so temporary structural untidiness improves concurrency without becoming permanent hidden debt or invisible checkpoint-era work","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:54.507814053Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:11.210888917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive-control","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","right-edge","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:54.507814053Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:38.081221818Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:58.906253068Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2292,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers WC.3, P1.5, P2.2-P2.7, DG.1, and O.4.
- This is the concrete right-edge/slack/phantom-leaf/deferred-cleanup bead for keeping inserts leaf-local longer.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.9","title":"I9: design version-gated parent micro-publish with local structural restart","description":"## Goal
Specify the first-wave optimistic structural protocol that replaces the already-falsified preclaim family.

## Scope
- define a protocol that reads a parent version token, computes split/sibling images privately, acquires the parent only for the final publish step, verifies the version, and releases immediately
- specify the parent version token or generation mechanism readable without long-lived exclusive ownership
- define local restart rules on mismatch so the whole transaction is not discarded unless semantically necessary
- spell out correctness, crash, and recovery constraints

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the protocol is specific enough to implement without reopening the markdown plan
- the design clearly states version-token semantics, publish-window boundaries, mismatch handling, and rollback/recovery expectations
- the design includes a step-by-step publish/restart sequence plus invariants that must hold before and after publish
- the design explicitly documents why it does not regress into longer shared ownership or universal waiting","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the protocol is specific enough to implement without reopening the markdown plan
- the design clearly states version-token semantics, publish-window boundaries, mismatch handling, and rollback/recovery expectations
- the design includes a step-by-step publish/restart sequence plus invariants that must hold before and after publish
- the design explicitly documents why it does not regress into longer shared ownership or universal waiting","notes":"Design-packet requirement:
- include a concrete sequence description for prepare, validate, publish, mismatch, and local-restart flows
- name the version anchors or generation counters involved, the exact point where shared ownership begins/ends, and the evidence hooks that later implementation/verification beads must expose
- explicitly compare the protocol against the rejected preclaim family so future agents do not reintroduce the same bad idea under new names
- state explicitly that any waiting policy here is narrow and structural-only for short publish windows; this bead must not become a Trojan horse for universal blocking waits on ordinary page conflicts
- record the local-restart boundary in enough detail that future agents can tell when restart stays local versus when true semantic invalidation forces wider restart","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:04.763900339Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:11.767502739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","design","micro-publish","mvcc","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:04.763900339Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:09.437778928Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:59.260884089Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:48.547656496Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:19.841786719Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2293,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers WC.2, NP.1, NP.4, NP.6, P3.1-P3.5, O.5, AVOID.1, AVOID.2, and AVOID.3.
- This is the design bead for the version-gated parent micro-publish protocol and local structural restart.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:43:58Z"}]}
